candidates.oracle: assert text_out == embeds_out -- text vs prompt_embeds with image_url must match under greedy
candidates.helper: aligned_content_and_embeds_b64 fixture (loads embed_tokens shard from Qwen2-VL-2B)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact string equality on LLM-generated text; no tolerance for numerical variation
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile can change floating-point numerics in multimodal embedding/attention, causing different greedy token selection
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - no specification requires text and prompt_embeds mixed with images to produce byte-identical output

candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - no specification requires text vs prompt_embeds representations to produce identical output when mixed with image_embeds

candidates.oracle: assert completion_mixed.choices[0].text == completion_embeds_only.choices[0].text -- mixed batch order matches individual
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact string equality on greedy-decoded LLM text; batch position should not affect output but no tolerance is given
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile can change batched computation order, padding, or attention numerics causing different greedy token selection across batch positions
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - batch position invariance is an implementation property, not an API specification; the API only guarantees each prompt gets a valid completion

candidates.oracle: assert completion_mixed.choices[0].text == completion_embeds_only.choices[0].text -- mixed batch order matches individual
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact string equality on greedy-decoded LLM text; batch position should not affect output but no tolerance is given
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile can change batched computation order, padding, or attention numerics causing different greedy token selection across batch positions
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - batch position invariance is an implementation property, not an API specification; the API only guarantees each prompt gets a valid completion

candidates.oracle: assert batch.choices[0].text == batch.choices[1].text -- two copies of same prompt must produce identical output
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact string equality on greedy-decoded LLM text across batch positions
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile can change batched computation padding or numerical path causing different greedy selection for same prompt at different batch positions
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - batch position invariance is an implementation property; the API only guarantees each prompt gets a valid completion, not that position in batch doesn't matter
candidates.code_snippet: assert batch.choices[0].text == batch.choices[1].text, "two copies of the same prompt should be the same"

candidates.oracle: assert batch.choices[0].text == batch.choices[2].text -- beam search: two copies of same prompt at different positions must match
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact string equality on beam-search-decoded text across batch positions
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile can alter beam search scoring numerics, changing which beam wins differently at different batch positions
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - batch position invariance under beam search is not an API guarantee
candidates.code_snippet: assert batch.choices[0].text == batch.choices[2].text, "two copies of the same prompt should be the same"

candidates.oracle: assert texts[0] == texts[1] -- streaming batch: two copies of same prompt must produce identical streamed text
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact string equality on streamed LLM text across batch positions
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile can change batched streaming computation path
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - batch position invariance is not an API spec guarantee

candidates.oracle: assert "12,742" in generated_text -- non-structured output must contain Earth diameter fact
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - checks for specific factual substring in LLM-generated text; the model could express this differently (e.g., 12742 without comma)
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile numerical changes could alter the model's phrasing of the fact
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - no contract requires the model to include this exact substring

candidates.oracle: check_output_matches_terms -- beam search output must contain semantic terms like 'boardwalk' + 'wooden'
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - checks for specific semantic terms in LLM output; model could describe scene differently
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - torch.compile numerical changes could alter beam search and change which descriptive terms the model uses
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - no contract requires specific descriptive terms to appear

candidates.oracle: Cross-TP output equality for quantized LoRA
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact text equality across TP configurations
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - PyTorch numeric changes could cause TP1 != TP2 if reduction order differs
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - no contract guarantees identical output across different TP configurations

candidates.comparison: exact text/token equality via check_outputs_equal (output_str_0 == output_str_1, output_ids_0 == output_ids_1) and exact logprob matching
candidates.oracle: Compares generation outputs and logprobs across different scheduling configs (async vs sync, preemption, executors, chunked prefill). Uses FP32 matmul precision, greedy decoding, flex_attention backend.
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES - exact text/token equality across configs. Even with FP32 and greedy, different scheduling paths can produce legitimately different token sequences due to batch composition effects on matmul numerics.
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES - PyTorch numeric changes (e.g., matmul precision, attention kernel changes) could cause divergence between the baseline and test configs even though both produce valid outputs.
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES - there is no mathematical guarantee that different scheduling/executor configs produce identical token sequences. The test assumes bitwise reproducibility across code paths that differ in batch layout and execution order.
